market_notes: Mid-April 2026: Nasdaq 10-day winning streak. SMH +13% 1W leading all benchmarks. AI infrastructure mega-rally extending. Iran war talks showing optimism — markets erasing war losses. Big bank earnings (JPM, C, GS, BlackRock) all beat on trading/M&A/capital markets. ASML reports April 15 (today), TSM April 16 (tomorrow). Software sector bouncing broadly — CRWD, SNOW, OKTA all rebounding. AVGO got fresh Meta custom AI chip deal (Reuters). ORCL up 14% on Bloom Energy fuel cell power deal for data centers (2.33x vol ratio). BE up 61% 1W near ATH on AI data center power demand. SNDK superbull emerges with '175% upside' thesis from Barrons. Memory storage supercycle in full swing. Iran cease-fire negotiations — energy names weakening (XOM -8.9% 1W). Software sector bounce is key risk to ZS short.
watching: ASML earnings TODAY April 15 — holding 1 share at +15.9%; watching for China/MATCH Act guidance and Q2 order book commentary; if disappoints on China exit = consider covering the 1 share
TSM earnings TOMORROW April 16 — holding 13 shares at +7.7%; Q1 pre-announced +35%; CRITICAL: Q2 guidance and AI capex commentary — strong guidance = consider adding, weak = trim to 8 shares
SNDK Nasdaq-100 inclusion effective — 7 shares at +22.1%; Barrons '175% upside' thesis; earnings April 30; watching for passive buying pressure peak
ZS short — software sector bouncing broadly (CRWD, SNOW, OKTA all up); watching whether ZS breaks above $132 stop or continues decline; AI disruption thesis vs Wedbush 'buy the dip' narrative
AVGO Meta custom AI chip deal — 13 shares total after today's add; watching for analyst PT upgrades following Reuters confirmation
ORCL data center power play — new 10-share position; watching for continuation of 2.33x vol ratio signal and any analyst upgrades on Bloom Energy deal
MRVL Google TPU talks — '24/7 Wall St says must-buy AI stock'; Cantor Fitzgerald and Barclays turned more positive; watching for formal deal announcement
Iran war talks — 'optimism' headline today; watching whether cease-fire materializes and impacts energy/defense names
NFLX earnings April 16 — not held; watching subscriber growth and AI content commentary as software/streaming sector read-through
Software sector bounce sustainability — NOW reports April 22, TSLA April 22, VRT April 22; watching whether bounce becomes rotation or just short squeeze

recent_runs.reasoning: **Macro:** Markets rallied strongly on Iran war de-escalation signal (Trump claims resolution in 2-3 weeks); oil retreated from >$100, risk-on tone returned. SPY flat 1W but Q1 close positive; QQQ and SMH rebounding after corrections. Flight-to-quality thesis intact but immediate macro tailwind reducing defensive urgency. Fed signals still pending—key inflection point. **Sectors:** Tech rotation accelerating into quality mega-caps (NVDA, ARM surging on AI optimism); semiconductor/infrastructure beneficiaries outperforming broader correction. Geopolitical relief lift broadening beyond quality into cyclicals (energy, financials). **Positions:** Portfolio is 82% cash, heavily underdeployed given rally + technical breakouts. ARM +12.1% 1W, MRVL +7.2% 1W, TSEM consolidating near $175 resistance are all tracking thesis. AKAM flat but thesis intact. ASML down -5.6% 1W but order book strength remains secular tailwind. No urgent exits; market inflection suggests incremental deployment opportunity into validated names + new entry setup in beaten-down AI infrastructure. Recent META short closure realized 8.8% loss but freed capital for offensive positioning.

**Macro:** Risk-on tone is accelerating as Iran de-escalation narrative strengthens (Trump speech imminent), oil retreating from highs, and broad equity rally continuing into Q2. SPY/QQQ/SMH all showing positive near-term momentum off Q1 lows. Earnings season begins with major banks (JPM, GS, C, MS) reporting April 13-16 and TSM April 16 — these will be critical macro catalysts for repricing. Semiconductor equipment and AI infrastructure thesis remains intact with MRVL/NVDA partnership news driving institutional confidence. **Sectors:** Semiconductor/AI infrastructure leading (MRVL +8.4% 1W, ARM +27.4% 1M, TSEM +49% 1M outperforming SMH). Financials showing relative strength ahead of earnings cycle; energy pulling back on Iran de-escalation (CVX -3.8% 1W, oil retreat). Optical/photonics catching a bid (COHR, LITE surging today on datacom demand). Defense names mixed. **Positions:** Portfolio is +0.8% overall with all 8 positions profitable — ARM +7.6%, MRVL +10.6%, TSEM +8.8%, ASML +3.8%, AMAT +3.5%, DOCN +2.2%, NVDA +0.8%, AKAM +1.1%. The portfolio is approximately 32% deployed ($32.7K long) with $68K cash (~66% cash). This is too conservative given: (1) all positions working, (2) risk-on environment accelerating, (3) earnings season creating actionable catalysts. Need to deploy capital into high-conviction setups. Key opportunities: MRVL has strong institutional momentum from NVIDIA partnership (BofA price target raise just now) — can add; MU just reported massive beat (+9% today) validating memory supercycle; TSM reporting April 16 with strong setup; COHR optical networking surge is directly tied to AI infrastructure buildout. AKAM negative coverage ('3 reasons to avoid') and weakest thesis in portfolio — consider trimming to redeploy capital. DOCN at 50% fwd P/E with limited near-term catalyst relative to alternatives. Upcoming earnings watchlist: TSM April 16 is a must-watch foundry bellwether. APLD reports April 8 — negative earnings expected, not holding. Banks reporting mid-April could lift financials. Need to add 3-4 positions to reach ~50% deployment while maintaining quality and avoiding overconcentration in semiconductor theme.

**Macro:** Market tone has shifted decisively bearish on the Iran variable — Trump has issued warnings of hitting Iran 'hard' rather than de-escalation, reversing the risk-on narrative from last week. Oil has spiked sharply, and chip stocks are falling (headline: 'Chip Stocks Fall After Trump Iran Warning'). This is a significant macro headwind that changes my positioning calculus. SPY +1.7% 1W, QQQ +1.9% 1W, SMH +3.0% 1W show the week is still positive, but the Iran escalation news is fresh and creating volatility. US lawmakers proposing crackdown on chip tool sales to China is another semiconductor headwind affecting AMAT, LRCX, ASML specifically. Trump also just slapped 100% tariffs on some drug imports per WSJ/Barrons — major pharma headwind. **Sectors:** Semiconductors facing dual headwinds (Iran risk + China export crackdown news). Energy re-surging on Iran war fears — oil spiking. Optical networking (CIEN +15.4% 1W, LITE +20.1% 1W) remains explosive. Gold continuing to perform well (NEM +14.8% 1W). Banks (GS, JPM, C) still intact ahead of earnings April 13-16 but BofA warns retail brokers face lower engagement from Middle East conflict. Defense names mixed but RTX/NOC getting attention. **Positions:** Portfolio has 10 positions, ~42% deployed with ~$58.6K cash. Key concerns: (1) AMAT is directly in crosshairs of China chip tool export crackdown — this is a material risk to my thesis; AMAT -2% revenue growth and fwd P/E 25x doesn't justify holding through regulatory headwinds. (2) ASML similarly exposed to China restriction headlines; however ASML earnings April 15 provides a catalyst to hold for. (3) TSEM has had a parabolic move (+15.1% unrealized, +60.2% 1M) and Jim Cramer specifically called for taking profits; GlobalFoundries patent suit is a lingering risk. With Iran escalation reversing the de-escalation thesis that supported risk-on deployment, I need to be more selective. (4) ARM is down -3.7% 1W now, having given back some gains; still +20.1% 1M. (5) MRVL is the strongest position at +7.3% with NVIDIA $2B endorsement intact — hold. (6) MU flat but thesis intact on HBM supercycle; Mizuho says selloff overdone. (7) TSM reporting April 16 — hold pre-earnings as expected beat. (8) NEM +14.8% 1W — gold is the best performer, hold with stop. (9) GS reporting April 13 — hold into earnings. (10) DOCN +4.9% but 51.6x fwd P/E with no near-term catalyst — weakest fundamental case. Actions: (a) SELL DOCN — weakest fundamental thesis (51.6x fwd P/E, limited near-term catalyst, Iran/macro uncertainty reduces risk appetite for speculative names); (b) SELL AMAT — China chip tool export crackdown is a direct fundamental headwind; -2% revenue growth already weak; (c) TRIM TSEM — take partial profits on 15.1% gain, parabolic move flagged by Cramer, patent litigation risk, keep 7 shares to maintain exposure; (d) ADD to CIEN or LITE — optical networking exploding with +15.4%/+20.1% 1W; CIEN at -1.2% from 52W high, earnings not near-term, AI infrastructure buildout thesis compelling; however LITE already up 20.1% in a week and 108% YTD — too extended. CIEN at fwd P/E 54.2x with +33% revenue growth and near 52W highs is interesting but expensive and already moved a lot; I'll pass for now and watch for pullback. (e) Consider adding defense exposure given Iran war escalation — RTX or NOC. RTX +1.7% 1W, fwd P/E 26.1x with 12% revenue growth and Iran war directly fueling defense contracts. Analysts are divided but Barrons notes 'Iran War Fuels Defense Contracts.' I'll add a small RTX position. (f) Consider energy play — oil spiking on Iran war; LNG gets Wall Street upgrade 'as Iran War Reshapes Global LNG Demand'; LNG -3.5% 1W but +12.9% 1M with fwd P/E 15.7x and strong LNG demand thesis. The long-term agent holds LNG at -7.1% — my thesis diverges (short-term Iran oil spike catalyst). I'll add a small LNG position. Net result: Reduce 3 positions (close DOCN, close AMAT, trim TSEM), add 2 new positions (RTX small, LNG small). Portfolio goes from 10 to 9 positions post-actions, with ~$58K+ cash base plus proceeds from sales. Cash management: After selling DOCN (~$2,250), AMAT (~$4,182), trimming TSEM sells 8 shares (~$1,582), total proceeds ~$8,014. After buying RTX 5 shares (~$981) and LNG 5 shares (~$1,406), net cash increases by ~$5,627. Final cash ~$64,243 / portfolio value ~$100,599, cash ~63.9%. This is elevated but justified given: Iran escalation reversal of risk-on thesis, China chip export headwinds hitting key holdings, bank earnings about to drop as major binary events. Cash is strategic dry powder for post-earnings deployment.

**Macro:** The macro regime is volatile with Iran war escalation as the primary swing factor — oil spiking, defense benefiting, but chip stocks under pressure from both Iran uncertainty and China export crackdown legislation. Benchmarks show SPY +1.7% 1W, QQQ +1.9% 1W, SMH +3.0% 1W — the week was positive but the Iran escalation is fresh. Strong jobs data out today is pushing back Fed cut expectations (bonds falling), which is a headwind for rate-sensitive growth names. Bank earnings begin Monday (GS April 13, JPM/C/JNJ April 14) — these are major binary events. **Sectors:** Optical networking (CIEN +15.4% 1W, LITE +20.1% 1W) remains explosive — both near 52W highs, AI infrastructure buildout thesis intact. Gold (NEM +14.8% 1W) is the strongest defense play. Semis are mixed: MRVL +9.7% 1W leading, TSM +4.0% 1W recovering, NVDA +3.6% 1W solid, but ARM -3.7% 1W giving back. Memory (SNDK +16.3% 1W, WDC +7.9% 1W) is surprisingly strong — AI storage supercycle narrative. Defense (RTX flat) not getting much bid yet despite Iran headlines. Energy (LNG -3.5% 1W) lagging despite oil spike — thesis not playing out quickly. INTC +14.2% 1W is an outlier — Apollo $14.2B deal catalyst. **Positions:** 9 open positions, ~36% deployed, ~64% cash. Working: MRVL +7.3% (NVIDIA $2B deal thesis intact, +9.7% 1W showing institutional accumulation), NEM +0.2% unrealized but +14.8% 1W strong (gold dual tailwind intact), ARM +3.5% (IBM deal confirms AI chip pivot thesis), TSEM +15.1% (parabolic but still near 52W high -0.6%). At risk: LNG flat (+0.0%) but -3.5% 1W — Iran war not translating to stock price; thesis slow; RTX flat — defense thesis not paying off yet, stock just matching SPY. TSM -0.7% reporting April 16 — hold. MU -0.4% slight lag. GS +0.3% into earnings Monday. ASML +0.6% with earnings April 15 and China restriction headwinds ongoing — Barron's specifically calls ASML most at risk from new House bill. Key decision points: (1) GS earnings Monday — hold small 4-share position; risk/reward reasonable. (2) ASML earnings April 15 — the new House bill specifically targeting ASML is a material risk; Billionaire Steve Cohen buying is bullish but legislative headwind is real. (3) TSM April 16 — strong setup, Cohen also buying. (4) LNG underperforming despite oil spike — reconsider. (5) Optical networking pullback opportunity — CIEN still near highs, not yet a pullback to add. New opportunities: INTC +14.2% 1W on Apollo deal is interesting but fwd P/E 50.8x with -4% revenue growth is not compelling fundamentally. SNDK +16.3% 1W, fwd P/E 8.0x, WDC +7.9% — memory storage thesis gaining momentum; SNDK at fwd P/E 8x with +61% revenue growth is compelling. GLW +9.3% 1W on Meta $6B AI fiber deal — optical networking adjacency, fwd P/E 37.9x not cheap but catalyst is fresh. NKE -15.1% 1W, -24.6% 1M — potential short? fwd P/E 22.6x, 0% revenue growth, -43.9% from 52W high, vol ratio 2.67x (unusual volume on selloff). AXON -8.4% 1W. Actions: (a) TRIM LNG — flat despite Iran thesis, -3.5% 1W, oil spike not translating; sell 3 shares to reduce risk while keeping small exposure for further escalation. (b) Add SNDK — memory storage AI supercycle, fwd P/E 8x on +61% rev growth, +16.3% 1W momentum, WDC AI storage narrative; small position 5 shares. (c) Consider SHORT NKE — -15.1% 1W, -24.6% 1M, -43.9% from 52W high, 0% revenue growth, vol ratio 2.67x (institutional distribution), fwd P/E 22.6x overvalued for zero-growth brand. However, bounces happen and I already have some shorts in recent history. NKE is at ~$44, very beaten up — the risk/reward on shorting something already -44% from highs is asymmetric vs mean reversion. Pass on short. (d) Hold ASML into earnings April 15 — Cohen buying is bullish signal, but China bill is specific headwind. Will hold 5 shares (small position, ~6.6% weight) and reassess post-earnings. (e) Hold GS into Monday earnings — small 4-share position, thesis intact on capital markets reactivation. Cash management: After selling 3 LNG (~$843) and buying 5 SNDK (~$3,508), net cash decreases by ~$2,665. Cash goes from $64,244 to ~$61,579. Still ~60% cash, justified as: major binary earnings events this week (GS Monday, ASML/MS/PNC Tuesday, TSM/NFLX/ABT/SCHW Wednesday), Iran macro uncertainty, and positioning for post-earnings deployment.

**Macro:** Market regime is risk-on with benchmarks surging: SPY +3.9% 1W, QQQ +4.6% 1W, SMH +5.8% 1W — a strong week driven by Iran cease-fire hopes (Trump offering 'olive branch' per IBD), strong jobs data, and broad AI infrastructure momentum. Oil at $110 with Strait of Hormuz closure fears creates cross-current, but equity markets are rallying on diplomatic breakthrough hopes. Bond yields rising (strong jobs pushes back Fed cuts) remains a headwind for rate-sensitive growth but not derailing the AI/semi rally. **Sectors:** Semiconductors leading (SMH +5.8% 1W) — MRVL +15.4% 1W is extraordinary, INTC +17.7% 1W on Apollo deal, AMD +9.0% 1W, NVDA +6.0% 1W. Memory storage exploding: SNDK +17.7% 1W, WDC +10.5% 1W. Optical networking (LITE +9.9%, CIEN +8.1%) continuing higher. Defense strong: RTX +4.6%, LMT +3.6%. Gold (NEM +10.5%) remains dual-tailwind play. Banks rallying into earnings: GS +7.9% 1W, C +9.3% 1W, JPM +4.5% 1W. Energy lagging (CVX -5.8%, XOM -4.5%, LNG -4.3%) as Iran cease-fire hopes reduce oil premium. **Positions:** Portfolio performing well — MRVL +9.7% unrealized is the star, TSEM +10.6%, MU +2.7%, SNDK +3.3%, ARM +3.2%, NVDA +1.9%. At risk: LNG -4.3% 1W as Iran de-escalation thesis hurts energy; NEM -0.8% unrealized but still +10.5% 1W strong. ASML flat to slightly negative with earnings April 15 and China export bill headwind. GS EARNINGS ALERT: GS reports April 13 (today/tomorrow). Holding 4 shares at +0.7% unrealized. BofA says banks set for in-line to better quarter — capital markets reactivation thesis intact, GS upgraded Netflix to Buy this week showing active dealmaking. Will HOLD into earnings — small 4-share position (~3.4% weight) limits binary risk, thesis intact on capital markets rebound. Key decisions: (1) LNG is now a problem — Iran cease-fire hopes are directly undermining the LNG war premium thesis, stock -4.3% 1W while energy sector broadly weak; CLOSE LNG. (2) TSEM at +10.6% unrealized and 39.7x fwd P/E with only +14% revenue growth — valuation stretched, GlobalFoundries patent suit risk, stock +10.6% 1W; TRIM to take profits. (3) MRVL is exceptional — +15.4% 1W, +44.7% 1M, new Buy initiation from Insider Monkey, NVIDIA $2B deal thesis, fwd P/E 20.3x on 22% growth is reasonable; HOLD and consider adding. (4) WDC +10.5% 1W — same AI storage thesis as SNDK; could add WDC as complement. (5) MPWR +12.3% 1W, Citi bullish, fwd P/E 45.7x on 21% growth — interesting but expensive. (6) AMD +9.0% 1W with Citi 30-day positive catalyst watch, fwd P/E 20.4x on 34% growth — compelling for swing trade. (7) UNH +8.6% 1W on Medicare rate boost — fwd P/E 14x on 12% growth with fresh FDA catalyst; interesting defensive/health play. (8) C +9.3% 1W reports April 14 — could add small pre-earnings position given BofA 'in-line to better quarter' call. (9) ASML earnings April 15 — holding 5 shares, small position; China export bill is headwind but Cohen buying bullish; HOLD small position. Actions: SELL LNG (thesis broken by Iran cease-fire), TRIM TSEM (take profits on 10.6% gain at stretched valuation), BUY AMD (Citi catalyst watch + strong momentum + reasonable valuation), BUY WDC (AI storage supercycle complement to SNDK, +10.5% 1W, fwd P/E 22.3x). **Cash:** Reducing cash from ~61% toward ~50% by deploying into high-conviction setups post-Iran de-escalation signal; maintaining dry powder for post-earnings deployment this week (JPM/C/JNJ April 14, ASML/MS/PNC April 15, TSM/NFLX April 16).

**Macro:** Iran cease-fire confirmed today (oil plunging, Dow +1,300 pts) — this is the most significant macro catalyst of the month, strongly risk-on. SPY +4.0% 1W, QQQ +5.0% 1W, SMH +10.3% 1W — semiconductors leading massively. The cease-fire removes the geopolitical premium from energy/defense and accelerates AI infrastructure rotation. **Sectors:** Semiconductors in full breakout (SMH +10.3% 1W) — MU +20.4% 1W, SNDK +22.9% 1W, WDC +25.2% 1W, MRVL +15.6% 1W all explosive. Optical networking (CIEN +27.3% 1W, LITE +27.5% 1W) parabolic. Defense mixed on cease-fire (LMT, NOC losing war premium). Energy getting crushed (XOM -7.9% 1W, CVX -6.8% 1W). Banks strong into earnings (C +8.9% 1W, JPM +5.2% 1W). **Earnings Alerts — REQUIRED:** GS reports April 13 (tomorrow): holding 4 shares at +5.3% unrealized. Schaeffer's asks 'Can Goldman deliver another post-earnings pop?' — capital markets reactivation thesis intact, BofA bullish on banks in-line/better quarter. GS +7.1% 1W into print, fwd P/E 13.9x on 15% revenue growth is reasonable. HOLD into earnings — small 4-share position (~3.6% weight) limits binary risk, and if capital markets beat, upside is meaningful. ASML reports April 15: holding 5 shares at +8.5% unrealized. Stock +7.6% 1W, MATCH Act spotlight on China revenue is a headwind but Iran cease-fire removes geopolitical risk premium discount. GuruFocus article 'ASML Set To Report Q1 Earnings: Here's What to Expect' suggests analyst expectations baked in. HOLD — Cohen buying bullish, cease-fire removes one headwind, but will monitor closely for guidance cut signals. **Positions:** Portfolio working well — MU +10.6%, SNDK +11.3%, WDC +11.4%, MRVL +14.6%, TSEM +18.5% are stars. ARM got Morgan Stanley downgrade (-1.6% 1W, stock 'sinks') — thesis weakening with 69.6x fwd P/E and chipmaking pivot concerns. RTX is losing defense premium as cease-fire removes Iran war catalyst; defense stocks mixed post cease-fire per Barrons. TSEM at +18.5% unrealized and 42.5x fwd P/E with 14% revenue growth — extremely stretched, taking profits. New opportunities: COHR +18.3% 1W, IBD 'New Buy' list includes GOOG/NVDA/VRT/AMAT post cease-fire. ANET +18.1% 1W with 29% revenue growth, fwd P/E 33.9x — AI networking backbone, vol ratio 1.87x showing institutional demand. ADI +8.8% 1W, Citi bullish into earnings. AVGO named top AI chip driver by BofA alongside NVDA/MRVL/AMD. Actions: (1) SELL RTX — cease-fire directly removes Iran war defense contract catalyst; defense stocks mixed post-announcement; lock in +3.7% gain. (2) SELL TSEM — take profits on +18.5% gain; 42.5x fwd P/E on 14% revenue growth extremely stretched; parabolic move needs digestion. (3) BUY ANET — AI networking infrastructure, +18.1% 1W, 1.87x vol ratio showing institutional accumulation, fwd P/E 33.9x on 29% revenue growth reasonable, 52W high -12% recovery potential. (4) BUY AVGO — BofA names as top AI chip driver, +13.3% 1W, fwd P/E 19.7x on 30% revenue growth, near 52W highs; strong conviction add. (5) ADD to NVDA — Iran cease-fire + BofA $1.3T chips forecast + IBD new buy list = triple catalyst; currently only 20 shares at modest weight. **Cash:** Reducing from 56% toward ~45% by deploying into high-conviction cease-fire beneficiaries and AI infrastructure names; maintaining dry powder for post-earnings TSM (April 16) and NFLX deployment opportunities.

**Macro:** Risk-on regime continues with SMH +9.8% 1W leading benchmarks, Iran cease-fire holding (with some doubt from Hormuz threats), and AI infrastructure mega-rally intact. Goldman just dropped a 'blunt warning on tech stocks' and Barron's notes 'Chip Stocks Are at Record Highs. Why That Feels a Wee Bit Much' — signs of near-term froth risk. EARNINGS ALERT REQUIRED RESPONSES: (1) GS reports April 13: Holding 4 shares at +5.1% unrealized. Financial stocks advancing into earnings, capital markets reactivation thesis intact, GS fwd P/E 13.9x on 15% revenue growth is reasonable. Goldman's own warning about tech froth is actually a signal they see rotation opportunity in financials. HOLD — small 4-share position limits binary risk, thesis intact. (2) ASML reports April 15: Holding 5 shares at +10.6% unrealized. Stock +6.5% 1W, strong outperformance. MATCH Act China exposure remains a headwind but Cohen buying bullish. At +10.6% unrealized with earnings in days, I will TRIM to 3 shares to lock in partial profit and reduce binary risk — the MATCH Act is a specific negative catalyst risk. (3) TSM reports April 16: Holding 8 shares at +7.0% unrealized. TSM is the foundry backbone for all AI chips — AI capex supercycle thesis intact, Citi bullish, fwd P/E 20.2x on 20% revenue growth and 45% margins is compelling. HOLD and plan to ADD post-earnings if guidance is strong. **Sectors:** Semiconductors and AI infrastructure dominating (MRVL +12.4% 1W, MU +14.6% 1W, SNDK +22.9% 1W, AMD +12.6% 1W). Memory storage supercycle in full swing — SNDK at +21.4% unrealized is the portfolio star. Optical networking (CIEN +17.3% 1W, VIAV +21.1% 1W, GLW +19.3% 1W) continuing to explode. Banks strong into earnings week. Software getting crushed on AI agent fears (NOW -13.7% 1W, SNOW -13.6% 1W, ZS -10.6% 1W, OKTA -14.4% 1W, NET -6.0% 1W) — this is a short opportunity. UNH surging +12.0% 1W on Medicare advantage payment hike — missed opportunity. **Positions:** Portfolio at +4.1% total P&L. Stars: SNDK +21.4%, MRVL +20.1%, MU +14.6%, WDC +11.1%, ASML +10.6%, AMD +7.5%, TSM +7.0%, NEM +4.6%. ARM -3.4% 1W, Morgan Stanley downgrade, 70x fwd P/E — thesis deteriorating. ANET +0.7% unrealized despite +17.0% 1W move means I entered near the top of the move. AVGO +1.2% unrealized, still early. Software sector meltdown (AI agent fears from Anthropic eating Palantir's lunch) creates short opportunities. NOW down -13.7% 1W and -23.0% 1M, -57.5% from 52W high, fwd P/E 17.9x on 21% growth — the AI agent disruption thesis could compress this further. SNOW -13.6% 1W, -52.9% from 52W high. Goldman's warning on tech froth is notable. ARM at 70x fwd P/E with Morgan Stanley downgrade and chipmaking pivot concerns — weakening thesis, consider trimming. Actions: (1) TRIM ASML — sell 2 of 5 shares to lock in partial profit ahead of earnings binary, reduce position from 5 to 3 shares. MATCH Act is specific China revenue headwind, stock up 10.6% — prudent risk management. (2) TRIM ARM — MS downgrade, 70x fwd P/E, -3.4% 1W underperforming all benchmarks. Sell 15 of 35 shares to reduce but maintain some exposure. (3) SHORT NOW — ServiceNow down -13.7% 1W, -23% 1M, -57.5% from 52W high. AI agent fears from Anthropic specifically threatening ServiceNow's workflow automation moat. Fwd P/E still 17.9x on 21% growth but AI disruption narrative accelerating. Vol ratio 2.42x showing heavy institutional selling. Reports April 22 — earnings risk but thesis is pre-earnings momentum short. (4) SHORT ZS — down -10.6% 1W, -21.9% 1M, -63.7% from 52W high. CrowdStrike and cybersecurity stocks getting crushed — Anthropic AI narrative hitting the sector. Vol ratio 2.71x showing massive distribution. fwd P/E 26.6x with -2% margins and -4% ROE. Cybersecurity software facing AI disruption narrative headwind. **Cash:** Holding ~50% cash (after trimming ASML and ARM reduces long exposure slightly, shorts add margin requirements) as strategic dry powder for post-earnings TSM add on April 16 if guidance is strong, and maintaining buffer against Goldman's tech froth warning and Hormuz/Iran re-escalation risk.
